title: malus domestica subsp. cerasifera
intro: Malus domestica subsp. cerasifera, also known as the sour cherry or myrobalan plum, is a deciduous tree or shrub belonging to the Rosaceae family and is a variant of the domesticated apple. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Malus domestica subsp. cerasifera include its ability to support digestive health, reduce inflammation, and act as an antimicrobial agent. The herbal preparations of this plant are typically made from its bark, leaves, or fruit, which can be used to create teas, infusions, or extracts.
